name: minimal-critic-loop
hypothesis: cand-0009 already scores 0.9925 with 5 phases and ~2min sentinel overhead. Approaching the ceiling, the biggest remaining risk is over-engineering: each new phase adds token cost and latency for diminishing quality gains. Counter-proposal: collapse SENTINEL + CHALLENGE into a single MINIMAL_CRITIC phase that runs one combined pass (static-analysis gap scan + adversarial negative-requirement probing) in parallel with the evaluator reading its output. This trades the belt-and-suspenders coverage for lower overhead while keeping the same DoD density via a stricter planner budget (5→6 DoD/feature). Risk: composite could dip if critic misses what sentinel+adversary together caught. Reward: if it holds, we free budget for future phases.

changes.planner: Tighter: 6 DoD/feature + 2 negative requirements (was 5+2 in cand-0009).
changes.minimal_critic: MERGED PHASE: Replaces sentinel + adversary. Single pass does (a) static gap scan and (b) negative-requirement probing. Emits critic-findings.md with up to 5 items.
changes.generator: Same as cand-0009 but consumes critic-findings.md on retry rounds (replaces sentinel-dod.md and adversary-report.md).
changes.evaluator: Same scoring. Receives critic-findings.md for DoD cross-check.
changes.skill: 4-phase workflow: PLAN → BUILD → MINIMAL_CRITIC → EVAL. Saves ~1.5min per task vs cand-0009.

per_task_notes.search-007: 2 rounds — merged critic missed one async-cleanup DoD that sentinel+adversary pair had caught separately in cand-0009
per_task_notes.token_savings: ~18% cheaper per task on average (as hypothesized)
